bonuses.leadin: A band performed on top of forklifts that followed behind an animatronic one of these animals in Liverpool to mark its status as 2008’s European Capital of Culture. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A band performed on top of forklifts that followed behind an animatronic one of these animals in Liverpool to mark its status as 2008's European Capital of Culture. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these animals exemplified by La Machine’s La Princesse. In 2025, the Tate Modern celebrated its 25th anniversary by installing a large stainless steel sculpture of one of these animals in Turbine Hall.

A spider is depicted in Louise Bourgeois’s Maman, a version of which stands outside a museum of this name in Bilbao. A New York museum of this name displays art around a spiral ramp designed by Frank Lloyd Wright.

The version of Maman at the Guggenheim Bilbao is made of this material. Michelangelo’s Dying Slave inspired an 1877 sculpture in this material that was so lifelike, its artist was accused of casting a live model.

bonuses.leadin: Banksy replaced the head of a Winged Victory of Samothrace-like statue with one of these objects in a 2006 work titled [this object] “Angel.” For 10 points each:
parts: Name these objects. Westminster Council destroyed a Banksy work titled “One Nation Under [these objects],”while in another work Banksy painted the words “what are you looking at?” on a wall opposite one of these objects.
Although they do many types of street art installations, Banksy is best-known for this street art form also created by Jean-Michel Basquiat (“BAH-skee-aht”).

The artist SpY, often described as this country’s Banksy, attached 150 fake CCTV cameras to a wall in 2014. Jeff Koons’s topiary sculpture Puppy sits outside a museum in this country whose exterior is clad in titanium sheets.
answers: cameras [accept security cameras or CCTV cameras; accept One Nation Under CCTV or CCTV Angel]
Spain [or España; or Kingdom of Spain or Reino de España] (The museum is the Guggenheim Museum Bilbao, designed by Frank Gehry.)
